source_name: Code4rena Dyad Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: Missing enough exogenous collateral check in VaultManagerV2::liquidate makes the liquidation revert even if (DYAD Minted > Non Kerosene Value)
summary: The vulnerability is present in the VaultManagerV2::liquidate function where it doesn’t have any check for whether the vault has enough exogenous collateral leading to no liquidation even if the non-kerosene value is less than DYAD minted.
root_cause: missing validation or protection in Missing enough exogenous collateral check in VaultManagerV2::liquidate makes the liquidation revert even if (DYAD Minted > Non Kerosene Value), leaving the affected path without the required safety check
broken_invariants: the vault path must not allow the condition described by "Missing enough exogenous collateral check in VaultManagerV2::liquidate makes the liquidation revert even if (DYAD Minted > Non Kerosene Value)" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
state changes must remain internally consistent across all affected accounting buckets and external integrations
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: The vulnerability is present in the VaultManagerV2::liquidate function where it doesn’t have any check for whether the vault has enough exogenous collateral leading to no liquidation even if the non-kerosene value is less than DYAD minted.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Update the line 214 in VaultManagerV2.sol: - if (cr >= MIN_COLLATERIZATION_RATIO) revert CrTooHigh(); + if (cr >= MIN_COLLATERIZATION_RATIO && getNonKeroseneValue(id) >= dyad.mintedDyad(address(this), id)) revert CrTooHigh();
